case_brief: Angela Torres, a 38-year-old marketing director at Pinnacle Consumer Brands, was terminated after the company discovered she had been running a competing consulting business during working hours using company resources. Over eight months, Torres used company computers, design software licenses, and client contact lists to solicit and serve five external clients in direct competition with Pinnacle's consulting subsidiary. The activity was discovered when an IT security audit flagged large data transfers to personal cloud storage. Torres had signed a non-compete and confidentiality agreement at hire. She claims her termination was gender discrimination, noting that male executives 'have side businesses.' However, no male employee has been found running a business in direct competition using company resources. Torres had no prior discrimination complaints on file and filed no internal grievances before termination. Employment at-will in New York (2nd Circuit). The company followed a documented investigation process before terminating.
